userStories.title: Prisma schema + token encryption utility + VCS config
userStories.description: Create VcsConnection and VcsSyncLog Prisma models, extend Ticket and Project models, implement AES-256-GCM token encryption/decryption utility, and register vcsConfig to read encryption key and polling defaults from environment. This story lays the foundation for all subsequent VCS work.
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma schema defines VcsConnection model with fields: id, projectId (unique, cascade delete), provider, repoOwner, repoName, encryptedToken, syncMode, allowedAuthors (JSON string), pollingIntervalMs, webhookSecret, lastSyncedAt, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
Prisma schema defines VcsSyncLog model with fields: id, vcsConnectionId (foreign key, cascade delete), syncType, issuesSynced, issuesSkipped, errorMessage, startedAt, completedAt
Ticket model extended with nullable fields: externalVcsId, externalVcsUrl, vcsSyncedAt
Project model extended with one-to-one optional relation vcsConnection (cascade delete)
encryptToken(plaintext, masterKey) returns string in format 'iv:authTag:ciphertext' where each segment is hex-encoded
decryptToken(encryptToken(plaintext, masterKey), masterKey) returns original plaintext
decryptToken(tampered_ciphertext, masterKey) throws error on GCM integrity check failure
decryptToken(validCiphertext, wrongMasterKey) throws error
vcsConfig registers with namespace 'vcs' and provides encryptionKey, defaultPollingIntervalMs, githubApiUrl from environment variables

routing.reasoning: validated from LLM output

userStories.title: Provider interface + GitHub provider implementation
userStories.description: Define IVcsProvider interface with fetchIssues, fetchIssue, and testConnection methods. Implement GitHubProvider using GitHub REST API. Create provider factory function that returns GitHubProvider for 'github' or throws ValidationAppException for unsupported providers like 'gitlab'.
acceptanceCriteria: IVcsProvider interface defines fetchIssues(since?: Date): Promise<VcsIssue[]>
IVcsProvider interface defines fetchIssue(issueNumber: number): Promise<VcsIssue>
IVcsProvider interface defines testConnection(): Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
VcsIssue type exports: number, title, body (nullable), authorLogin, url, labels, createdAt
GitHubProvider.fetchIssues() calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with query params: state=open, sort=created, direction=asc
GitHubProvider.fetchIssues(since) passes ISO 8601 since parameter to filter issues created after date
GitHubProvider.fetchIssues() filters out entries where pull_request field is present (API returns PRs in issues endpoint)
GitHubProvider.fetchIssue(42) calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/42 and returns VcsIssue
GitHubProvider.fetchIssue(nonexistent) throws NotFoundAppException when GitHub returns 404
GitHubProvider.testConnection() returns { ok: true } when token has repo read access
GitHubProvider.testConnection() returns { ok: false, error: 'message' } when token invalid or repo not found
createVcsProvider('github', config) returns GitHubProvider instance
createVcsProvider('gitlab', config) throws ValidationAppException indicating unsupported provider

routing.reasoning: validated from LLM output

userStories.title: IVcsProvider interface + VcsIssue type definitions
userStories.description: Define the IVcsProvider interface and VcsIssue type that all VCS provider implementations must satisfy. These are pure TypeScript contracts with no runtime logic.
acceptanceCriteria: VcsIssue type exports fields: number (issue number), title (string), body (string | null), authorLogin (string), url (string), labels (string[]), createdAt (Date)
IVcsProvider interface defines fetchIssues(since?: Date): Promise<VcsIssue[]>
IVcsProvider interface defines fetchIssue(issueNumber: number): Promise<VcsIssue>
IVcsProvider interface defines testConnection(): Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>
Both VcsIssue and IVcsProvider are exported from a shared types or vcs-provider module within apps/api/src

routing.reasoning: Pure TypeScript type and interface definitions, no runtime logic, single file, under 30 LOC.

userStories.title: GitHubProvider implementation
userStories.description: Implement GitHubProvider class that satisfies IVcsProvider. Uses GitHub REST API to fetch issues and test connectivity. Filters pull requests from the issues endpoint response.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHubProvider.fetchIssues() calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with state=open, sort=created, direction=asc and filters out entries where pull_request field is present
GitHubProvider.fetchIssues(since) passes ISO 8601 since parameter to the GitHub issues endpoint
GitHubProvider.fetchIssue(issueNumber) calls GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{issueNumber} and returns a mapped VcsIssue
GitHubProvider.fetchIssue(nonexistent) throws NotFoundAppException when GitHub returns 404
GitHubProvider.testConnection() returns { ok: true } when token has repo read access
GitHubProvider.testConnection() returns { ok: false, error: 'message' } when the token is invalid or repo is not found
All HTTP requests include Authorization: Bearer {token} header
GitHub API responses are mapped to VcsIssue shape (number, title, body, authorLogin, url, labels, createdAt)

routing.reasoning: Multiple methods with HTTP interaction, edge case handling (404, PR filtering, since param), and response mapping across ~120-160 LOC. Fits three-session-tdd-lite with mocked HTTP in tests.

userStories.title: createVcsProvider factory function
userStories.description: Implement a factory function createVcsProvider(providerType, config) that returns a GitHubProvider for 'github' or throws ValidationAppException for unsupported provider types.
acceptanceCriteria: createVcsProvider('github', config) returns a GitHubProvider instance
createVcsProvider('gitlab', config) throws ValidationAppException indicating the provider is unsupported
createVcsProvider with any unrecognised providerType throws ValidationAppException
Factory is exported and usable by the sync service (VCS-P1-004)

routing.reasoning: Single function, switch/if pattern, under 20 LOC. Isolated from HTTP logic. Simple enough for tdd-simple.

userStories.title: VCS connection CRUD API (service + controller + DTOs)
userStories.description: Implement VcsConnectionService with create, read, update, delete operations that handle token encryption and validation. Create VcsController with REST endpoints for connection management. Define DTOs for request/response serialization. Token is never returned in API responses.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/:slug/vcs with valid CreateVcsConnectionDto returns 201 with VcsConnectionResponseDto (token field omitted)
POST /projects/:slug/vcs encrypts plaintext token before storage (raw token never persisted)
POST /projects/:slug/vcs returns 409 when project already has a connection
POST /projects/:slug/vcs returns 404 when project slug not found
GET /projects/:slug/vcs returns VcsConnectionResponseDto with all fields except token
GET /projects/:slug/vcs returns 404 when no connection exists
PATCH /projects/:slug/vcs with { syncMode: 'polling' } updates sync mode and returns updated VcsConnectionResponseDto
PATCH /projects/:slug/vcs with { token: 'new-token' } decrypts old token, re-encrypts new token, and stores
DELETE /projects/:slug/vcs returns 204 and removes connection
DELETE /projects/:slug/vcs returns 404 when no connection exists
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/test decrypts stored token, calls provider.testConnection(), returns TestConnectionResultDto

routing.reasoning: validated from LLM output

userStories.title: VCS connection DTOs and response serialization
userStories.description: Define all request and response DTOs for VCS connection endpoints: CreateVcsConnectionDto, UpdateVcsConnectionDto, VcsConnectionResponseDto (token excluded), and TestConnectionResultDto. Use class-validator decorators for input validation.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateVcsConnectionDto requires provider (enum), token (string), repoUrl (string), and optional syncMode and webhookSecret fields
UpdateVcsConnectionDto has all fields optional: token, syncMode, webhookSecret
VcsConnectionResponseDto includes all VcsConnection fields except token
TestConnectionResultDto includes success (boolean), latencyMs (number), and optional error (string)
CreateVcsConnectionDto rejects invalid provider enum values with a validation error
CreateVcsConnectionDto rejects missing required fields with a validation error

routing.reasoning: Pure DTO definitions with class-validator decorators; under 50 LOC, single-purpose files, no business logic

userStories.title: VcsConnectionService CRUD with token encryption
userStories.description: Implement VcsConnectionService with create, findByProject, update, and delete operations. Token must be encrypted before storage using the utility from VCS-P1-001 and never returned in query results. Update re-encrypts token when provided. testConnection() decrypts the stored token and delegates to the provider.
acceptanceCriteria: create() encrypts the plaintext token using the encryption utility before persisting to the database
create() throws ConflictException (409) when the project already has a VCS connection
create() throws NotFoundException (404) when the project slug does not resolve to a project
findByProject() returns the connection record with the token field omitted
findByProject() throws NotFoundException (404) when no connection exists for the project
update() with syncMode updates only the syncMode field and returns the updated record without token
update() with a new token decrypts the current stored token, re-encrypts the new token, and persists it
delete() removes the connection record and returns void
delete() throws NotFoundException (404) when no connection exists for the project
testConnection() decrypts the stored token, calls provider.testConnection() via createVcsProvider factory, and returns TestConnectionResultDto

routing.reasoning: Security-critical: handles token encryption/decryption and storage; spans Prisma, encryption utility, and provider factory; three-session-tdd required by security override

userStories.title: VcsController REST endpoints for connection management
userStories.description: Implement VcsController with REST endpoints under /projects/:slug/vcs that delegate to VcsConnectionService. Wire HTTP status codes, guard routes, and apply the response DTO transformation. Includes the POST /test sub-route.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/:slug/vcs with valid CreateVcsConnectionDto returns 201 with VcsConnectionResponseDto
POST /projects/:slug/vcs propagates 409 ConflictException when project already has a connection
POST /projects/:slug/vcs propagates 404 NotFoundException when project slug is not found
GET /projects/:slug/vcs returns 200 with VcsConnectionResponseDto when connection exists
GET /projects/:slug/vcs returns 404 when no connection exists for the project
PATCH /projects/:slug/vcs returns 200 with updated VcsConnectionResponseDto
DELETE /projects/:slug/vcs returns 204 with no body when deletion succeeds
DELETE /projects/:slug/vcs returns 404 when no connection exists
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/test returns 200 with TestConnectionResultDto

routing.reasoning: Standard NestJS controller wiring; delegates all logic to the service; 2-3 files, clear patterns matching existing controllers in the codebase

userStories.title: Issue sync core + polling + webhook + manual import
userStories.description: Implement core sync logic (vcsService.syncIssue) shared by all three sync paths. Set up polling via SchedulerRegistry with dynamic interval management. Implement webhook receiver with HMAC signature verification. Add manual single-issue import endpoint. Write sync logs for audit trail. Handle filtering by allowedAuthors, deduplication, error cases.
acceptanceCriteria: vcsService.syncIssue(project, issue, 'manual') creates ticket with type='TASK', status='CREATED', priority='MEDIUM'
vcsService.syncIssue() returns { action: 'skipped' } when ticket with matching externalVcsId exists in project
vcsService.syncIssue() sets externalVcsId, externalVcsUrl, vcsSyncedAt on newly created ticket
vcsService.syncIssue() allocates ticket.number via MAX(number)+1 in Prisma transaction
VcsSyncService registers interval via SchedulerRegistry for each connection with syncMode='polling' on module initialization
Polling service filters issues by allowedAuthors before calling syncIssue()
Polling service updates connection.lastSyncedAt only after successful sync run
Polling service writes VcsSyncLog entry with issuesSynced, issuesSkipped, startedAt, completedAt after each run
Polling fetch failure writes VcsSyncLog with errorMessage, does not update lastSyncedAt, and retries on next interval
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook with valid X-Hub-Signature-256 and issues.opened event syncs issue
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook with invalid X-Hub-Signature-256 returns 401
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook with issues.closed event returns 200 { ignored: true }
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook filters by allowedAuthors, returns 200 { ignored: true } for non-allowed authors
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync/:issueNumber fetches issue and creates ticket regardless of allowedAuthors
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync/:issueNumber returns 409 when issue already synced
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync triggers full sync run and returns SyncResultDto with counts and created ticket refs

routing.reasoning: validated from LLM output

userStories.title: Core vcsService.syncIssue with deduplication and ticket creation
userStories.description: Implement the shared vcsService.syncIssue(project, issue, source) method used by all three sync paths. Creates a ticket from a VcsIssue, deduplicates via externalVcsId, sets VCS metadata fields, and allocates ticket.number atomically via MAX(number)+1 inside a Prisma transaction.
acceptanceCriteria: syncIssue(project, issue, 'manual') creates a ticket with type='TASK', status='CREATED', priority='MEDIUM'
syncIssue() returns { action: 'created', ticketId } on successful creation
syncIssue() returns { action: 'skipped' } when a ticket with the same externalVcsId already exists in the project
Created ticket has externalVcsId, externalVcsUrl, and vcsSyncedAt populated from the VcsIssue input
ticket.number is allocated as MAX(number)+1 scoped to the project inside a single Prisma transaction, preventing duplicates under concurrent requests

routing.reasoning: Core shared logic touching Prisma transactions, ticket creation invariants, and deduplication. ~180 LOC across service + unit tests. All downstream sync paths depend on this.

userStories.title: Polling service with SchedulerRegistry, allowedAuthors filter, and VcsSyncLog
userStories.description: Implement VcsSyncService that registers a polling interval per VCS connection with syncMode='polling' on module init using NestJS SchedulerRegistry. Each poll fetches issues from the provider, filters by allowedAuthors, calls syncIssue for each candidate, updates lastSyncedAt on success, and writes a VcsSyncLog entry with issue counts. On fetch failure, writes an error log without updating lastSyncedAt.
acceptanceCriteria: VcsSyncService registers one SchedulerRegistry interval per connection with syncMode='polling' on module initialization
Polling run filters fetched issues against connection.allowedAuthors before calling syncIssue()
Polling run updates connection.lastSyncedAt only after all syncIssue calls complete without a fetch error
Polling run writes a VcsSyncLog entry with issuesSynced, issuesSkipped, startedAt, and completedAt on success
Polling run writes a VcsSyncLog entry with errorMessage and does not update lastSyncedAt when the provider fetch throws

routing.reasoning: Integrates SchedulerRegistry with dynamic interval management, allowedAuthors filtering, audit logging, and error-path divergence. ~220 LOC across service + integration tests.

userStories.title: Webhook receiver with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification and event routing
userStories.description: Implement POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook controller and handler. Verify the X-Hub-Signature-256 header using HMAC-SHA256 against the connection's webhookSecret before processing any payload. Route issues.opened events to syncIssue() with allowedAuthors filtering. Return 200 { ignored: true } for issues.closed and non-allowed authors. Return 401 for invalid signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook with a valid X-Hub-Signature-256 and issues.opened event calls syncIssue() and returns the sync result
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook with an invalid X-Hub-Signature-256 returns HTTP 401 without processing the payload
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook with issues.closed event returns HTTP 200 { ignored: true }
POST /projects/:slug/vcs-webhook filters the issue author against allowedAuthors and returns HTTP 200 { ignored: true } for non-allowed authors
HMAC verification uses timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks

routing.reasoning: Security-critical: HMAC verification requires three-session-tdd regardless of LOC. Controller + service + timing-safe crypto ~200 LOC. Must enforce security override.

userStories.title: Manual single-issue and full-sync endpoints
userStories.description: Add two manual sync endpoints to VcsController. POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync/:issueNumber fetches a single issue by number from the provider and creates a ticket, bypassing allowedAuthors. Returns 409 if already synced. POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync triggers a full sync run and returns a SyncResultDto with issue counts and created ticket references.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync/:issueNumber fetches the issue from the provider and calls syncIssue() regardless of allowedAuthors
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync/:issueNumber returns HTTP 409 when the issue is already synced (externalVcsId exists)
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync/:issueNumber returns the created ticket reference on success
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync triggers a full provider fetch, calls syncIssue() for each issue, and returns SyncResultDto with issuesSynced and issuesSkipped counts
POST /projects/:slug/vcs/sync includes created ticket IDs in SyncResultDto createdTickets array

routing.reasoning: Two REST endpoints with straightforward delegation to syncIssue() and the polling run logic. ~150 LOC. No new abstractions; primary risk is the 409 conflict path and the SyncResultDto shape.

analysis:
## Key Architectural Changes

This feature introduces a per-project VCS abstraction layer that enables issue sync from GitHub (with GitLab support designed for Phase 5). The implementation spans three API layers: provider interface, connection management service, and sync orchestration service.

### New Prisma Models
- **VcsConnection**: Stores per-project VCS config (provider, repo coords, encrypted token, sync mode, allowed authors, webhook secret, polling state)
- **VcsSyncLog**: Audit trail for sync runs (type, counts, errors, timestamps)
- **Ticket extensions**: externalVcsId, externalVcsUrl, vcsSyncedAt (to track synced issues)
- **Project extensions**: one-to-one optional vcsConnection relation

Existing models (Ticket, Project, Webhook) will be extended, not replaced. Migrations will be additive.

### Security-Critical Components
1. **Token Encryption**: AES-256-GCM with per-encryption random IV. Master key from VCS_ENCRYPTION_KEY env. Decryption failure should mark connection inactive, not crash the server.
2. **Webhook Verification**: HMAC-SHA256 signature on X-Hub-Signature-256 header (existing pattern in webhook-dispatcher.service.ts)
3. **Auth on Endpoints**: Bearer token required for all non-public endpoints; webhook receiver is @Public() but signature-verified

### Provider Abstraction
IVcsProvider interface with three methods (fetchIssues, fetchIssue, testConnection) enables GitLab to be swapped in Phase 5 without changing sync logic. GitHub API quirks handled in GitHubProvider: filter pull requests from issue listing, handle 404 as NotFoundAppException.

### Sync Orchestration
Three entry points (polling interval, webhook, manual import) converge on vcsService.syncIssue(project, issue, syncType). Core logic:
1. Check if ticket with externalVcsId already exists (idempotency)
2. Create ticket with type=TASK, status=CREATED, priority=MEDIUM
3. Allocate number via MAX(number)+1 in transaction (existing pattern)
4. Set external tracking fields (externalVcsId, externalVcsUrl, vcsSyncedAt)
5. Filter by allowedAuthors (except manual import)

**Polling Service**: Uses SchedulerRegistry to register dynamic intervals per connection. Must handle:
- Module init: register intervals for existing active connections
- Create/update connection: add/replace interval with new config
- Delete connection: unregister interval
- Fetch failure: log error, write VcsSyncLog, retry on next interval (don't update lastSyncedAt)

**Webhook Handler**: Verifies HMAC signature, processes only issues.opened events, filters by allowedAuthors, returns 200 { ignored: true } for filtered/unsupported events.

**Manual Import**: Endpoint for importing single issue by number, bypasses allowedAuthors filter. Useful for one-off issues or testing.

### CLI + Web Integration
CLI uses generated OpenAPI client from apps/cli/src/generated/ (regenerate after API endpoint changes). Web uses Nuxt composables and proxying pattern (not generated client).

### Dependencies & Impact
- **Prisma**: Schema migration required (new models, extended models)
- **NestJS**: SchedulerRegistry for polling, DI for services, config registration
- **Fastify**: No changes needed
- **Commander.js**: New vcs subcommand group
- **Nuxt**: New /[project]/settings page
- **Existing services**: Ticket creation (number allocation), project resolution, auth

### Risks & Mitigations
1. **Rate Limiting**: GitHub API rate limit (60 req/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr authenticated). Mitigation: Respect Retry-After header, log warnings in VcsSyncLog, retry on next interval.
2. **Token Leakage**: Never log plaintext token, mask in API responses, store only encrypted. Use @Sensitive() if available.
3. **Duplicate Sync**: Multiple webhook hits or polling overlaps. Mitigation: unique(projectId, externalVcsId) check before sync.
4. **Scheduler Leaks**: Intervals not cleaned up on deletion. Mitigation: explicit unregisterAll + defensive checks.
5. **Decryption Failure**: Corrupted ciphertext or wrong key. Mitigation: mark connection inactive, log error, return 500 on sync attempts.
6. **Migration Path**: Add nullable VcsConnection fields first, migrate data separately, enable constraints in separate migration.

### Test Coverage Strategy
Each story enforces three-session TDD (test-writer → implementer → verifier) for security-critical and complex stories. Provider tests mock GitHub API. Sync tests use in-memory Prisma fixture. Polling tests verify SchedulerRegistry interaction. Webhook tests verify signature validation.
